description: Audit records live in a table the application can update or delete, so the trail can be silently rewritten.
body: Failure mode: the audit log is an ordinary table the application's own role can UPDATE/DELETE, or it is a log file that rotates and is editable, so a compromised or careless actor can alter or remove evidence without detection. Detection signals: the app role holds write/delete on the audit store; no hash chain or WORM policy; deleting a record leaves no trace. Do not store audit evidence where the audited application can modify it. Safer fallback: move records to append-only storage (WORM, hash-chained, or a separate-account sink), remove update/delete from the app role, and verify tampering is blocked or chain-detectable in a test.
